title: Spells System Index

overview.description: This chapter gives rules for casting spells and includes descriptions of common spells in the worlds of Dungeons & Dragons. Those spells are used by many class features, magic items, and monsters.
overview.scope: Comprehensive spellcasting mechanics covering preparation, casting, targeting, and effects

components.title: Core Spell Rules
components.description: Fundamental mechanics for spellcasting including spell levels, slots, and casting without slots
contains: Casting without slots (cantrips, rituals, special abilities, magic items)
Saving throw and attack roll calculations

components.title: Spell Preparation Rules
components.description: Class-specific rules for preparing and changing prepared spells
contains: Preparation by class table (8 spellcasting classes)
Change frequency (Long Rest vs Level Up)

components.title: Schools of Magic
components.description: The eight schools that categorize all spells

components.title: Spell Components and Casting
components.description: Physical and temporal requirements for spell casting
contains: Casting time types (Action, Bonus Action, Reaction, longer times)
One spell slot per turn rule

components.title: Spell Effects and Targeting
components.description: Rules for spell effects, targeting, and interaction mechanics
contains: Areas of effect (Cone, Cube, Cylinder, Emanation, Line, Sphere)
Combining different vs same spell effects

key_mechanics.one_slot_per_turn: You can expend only one spell slot to cast a spell per turn
key_mechanics.higher_level_casting: Spells cast with higher-level slots take on that higher level
key_mechanics.spell_identification_dc: DC 15 Intelligence (Arcana) check with Study action

casting_without_slots: Ritual casting (+10 minutes, must be prepared)
